name: submit_lesson_draft
description: Submit the final lesson draft as structured content. Schema is enforced by the LLM provider — invalid arguments are rejected before the tool fires, so you cannot produce malformed output if you submit through this tool. The Orchestrator reads only the structured arguments; do not duplicate content as plain text after the tool call. Cited symbols are sanity-checked against research_notes — invent a class/function name and the Reviewer will fail you with grounding fatal.

overview.description: 1-2 paragraph high-level introduction to what this lesson teaches. MUST mention the primary symbol by name and explain its role in the codebase. No code blocks here — pure prose.

how_it_works.description: Step-by-step explanation of the symbol's behavior, grounded in research_notes. May include code blocks with verbatim signatures (see Verbatim Citation Discipline). Use 1-3 paragraphs. If research_notes shows callees, explain the call sequence here.

key_details.description: Notable implementation details, edge cases in the body, parameter semantics, return type implications. Empty string "" if the symbol is trivial (single-line helper, plain getter) and there are no notable details.

what_to_watch_for.description: Edge cases, gotchas, dependencies the reader should pay attention to when reading or modifying this code. Include cross-references to other symbols by name when behavior depends on them. 1-2 paragraphs.

cited_symbols.description: Every class, function, method, or dataclass name referenced in code blocks across all sections. Each entry MUST appear verbatim in research_notes. Used by the Reviewer for fast grounding sanity check before deep rubric. Order does not matter.

symbol.description: Symbol name flagged UNCERTAIN in research_notes

callout.description: Brief explanation of why uncertain (dynamic dispatch, reflection, runtime polymorphism). Will be rendered as Obsidian > [!note] callout in the lesson.

uncertain_regions.description: Symbols flagged as UNCERTAIN in research_notes that you reference in any section. May be empty array if no uncertain symbols are referenced. Each entry will be rendered as a > [!note] callout block in the final lesson markdown.
